body.notes: From Opus reorg pass: 'IgorDevice has comms() but the router can't see the address it publishes'. This is a bootstrap blocker.
body.title: IgorDevice.comms() advertises address that router cannot resolve
body.result: Closed as side effect of T-announce-slice2-suffix-comms-routing — router now peels suffix variants so IgorDevice.comms() addresses resolve cleanly. agent_datacenter commit 0ed4a75.
body.description:
router.py:71 strips comms:// scheme to a flat mailbox name, but IgorDevice.comms() advertises comms://igor/inbox while skeleton creates the bare 'igor' mailbox. resolve('comms://igor/inbox') raises AddressError because 'igor/inbox' is not in known mailboxes. Per design § 2 + § 14 (11.A): resolve via suffix-style addressing (comms://igor.inbox) — same flat-mailbox invariant, no router parser change. Fix lands as part of announce-protocol v1 sprint (introduces the surface mailboxes).

**Affected files:** /home/akien/dev/src/agent_datacenter/devices/igor/device.py:101 (comms() returns wrong shape), /home/akien/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/skeleton/skeleton.py:125 (mailbox creation), /home/akien/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/bus/router.py:71 (resolve)
**Design rules:** suffix-style-addressing (§ 14 11.A ratified)
**Scope boundary:** in — change comms() to return suffix-style address; ensure skeleton creates matching mailbox(es) for advertised surfaces; out — router parser change (path-style was rejected)
**Test plan:** test_comms_address_resolves_after_register (round-trip register → comms() → resolve → mailbox exists); regression for IgorDevice and ClaudeDevice
